reasoning: The question asks if the federal funds target range upper limit will be higher than its 2025-10-26 value (which is 4.25%) on three specific dates: 2025-11-02, 2025-11-25, and 2026-01-24. The next scheduled FOMC meetings are October 28-29, 2025, and December 9-10, 2025. Market expectations, according to CME FedWatch and recent Reuters polling (October 2025), show a near-100% chance of a rate cut at the October meeting, and strong expectations for another cut in December 2025. Because the Fed is in a definitive easing cycle with multiple cuts expected before the end of the year, the likelihood of a rate *hike* over the baseline by any of the resolution dates is remarkably low. The very small residual probability accommodates the tail risk of an unexpected, severe inflationary shock leading to an emergency rate reversal, with this uncertainty naturally increasing slightly over the longer forecast horizons.
